name: Passage
description: A complete unit of narrative prose ending in one or more player-facing choices. Passages are the atomic reading units of the manuscript, written by Scene Smith from Passage Briefs. Passages are drafted in workspace (draft/review/approved states), then Gatekeeper promotes approved passages to manuscript store (cold state). In Cold state, passages are player-safe and ready for export. Terminology aligned with Twine/Twee for export compatibility.

fields.name: pid
fields.description: Passage identifier (unique within the story, e.g., 'intro', 'kitchen-discovery')

fields.name: brief_ref
fields.description: Reference to the Passage Brief this implements (format: PB-NNN)

fields.name: topology_passage_ref
fields.description: Reference to the topology passage this implements (must match topology.passages[].pid)

fields.name: prose
fields.description: Narrative paragraphs with opening beat, development, and setup for choices. 150-500 words typical. May contain inline codex references as bold text.

fields.name: choices
fields.description: Player-facing choices at the end of the passage. Minimum 2, maximum 5.

properties.name: text
properties.description: Choice text shown to player (concrete verb + object)

properties.name: target
properties.description: PID this choice leads to (must match a topology link)

properties.name: condition_type
properties.description: Type of gate: token, reputation, knowledge, tool

properties.name: diegetic_check
properties.description: In-world phrasing for the gate (what PN says)

properties.name: diegetic_refusal
properties.description: In-world phrasing when gate blocks access

fields.name: codex_refs
fields.description: List of codex entry IDs referenced in this passage

properties.name: art_plan_ref
properties.description: Reference to Art Plan if exists

properties.name: audio_plan_ref
properties.description: Reference to Audio Plan if exists

fields.name: convergence_notes
fields.description: If this passage converges from multiple paths, notes for immediate reflection

states.name: Draft
states.description: LLM agents actively working on content

states.name: Review
states.description: Studio work complete; ready for human review

custom_rules.description: Choices must be contrastive (different outcomes), not synonyms
No internals, codewords, gate logic, or spoilers in prose or choice text
All gates must use in-world phrasing (token, reputation, knowledge, tool)
All choice targets must match links defined in the topology for this passage
